mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending confidence and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5% threshold, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) indicate a contraction in real economic output, creating negative SPX forward returns over 60 trading days through reduced corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce negative returns in > 40% of cases where both INDPRO and GDPC1 decline, or the interaction effect's IC is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day surge in housing starts (HOUST) above its 12-month moving average signals strong demand for residential construction, driving positive SPX returns through sectoral momentum and improved consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances where HOUST exceeds its 12mo MA, or the signal's IC falls below 0.02 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative SPX forward returns over 20 trading days through higher equity discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3%, or the signal's IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) below 100k indicates weak labor market growth, reducing corporate hiring and consumer spending, which generates negative SPX returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS falls below 100k, or the signal's IC drops below -0.03 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP (GDPC1) reflects weakening demand, creating negative SPX returns over 20 trading days through reduced corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce negative returns in > 40% of cases where both RSAFS and GDPC1 decline, or the interaction effect's IC is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals weakening supply chain capacity, generating negative SPX returns over 5 trading days through sectoral earnings disappointments.
failure_condition: SPX returns are positive in > 55% of instances where INDPRO falls below its 12mo MA, or the signal's IC is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).
